audit_items.proof_trace: All sk_buff allocations in tls.c (the implementation of functions declared in crypto.h) are freed on every execution path. `__nfp_ccm_mbox_communicate` invariably frees the skb on error (ccm_mbox.c:592-593), callers free on success via `dev_consume_skb_any`; `nfp_net_tls_add` uses `skb_get` intentionally to maintain refcount during key-zeroing, with all error labels (err_free_skb, err_conn_remove) correctly releasing memory. `nfp_net_tls_rx_resync_req` performs no heap allocation and releases sock references via `sock_gen_put` on all paths.
